sprint: Sprint 32 -- V0.27.6 Bug Fixes (Spool 2026-05-11 audit: pymysql + sqlite fallback + orphan cleanup + stranded backfill + drive_statistics writer)
spec: offices/pm/inbox/archive/2026-05/2026-05-11-from-spool-calibration-cli-pymysql-missing.md (Spool's 5-story audit: A pymysql / B sqlite fallback / C orphan cleanup / D backfill / E drive_statistics writer gap)
backlogItem: Bug-fix sprint #5 in the V0.27 chain. Per CIO 2026-05-11 directive 'keep story A and proceed with all user stories that are bug fixes in this sprint' -- all 5 Spool audit findings included. Per chain-end-merge rule: V0.27.6 branches FROM sprint/sprint31-bugfixes-V0.27.5 (cumulative chain), NOT from main. CIO Option A pick implicit on Story B (remove sqlite fallback; PM + Spool joint recommendation; CIO can redirect mid-sprint).

testBaseline.note: Post-V0.27.5 baseline (V0.27.5 sprint-tip @ bb744d1). Verify exact count at sprint start.

sprintNotes: GOAL: Close 5 bugs from Spool's 2026-05-11 calibration-CLI audit. US-320 pymysql missing (CLI universally crashes). US-321 sqlite fallback phantom (crashes when used). US-322 Pi realtime_data NULL-drive_id orphan cleanup (61,293 rows accumulated since Session 10). US-323 server battery_health_log backfill 11-15 (stranded pre-V0.27.4-US-315). US-324 drive_statistics writer (NO writer exists; calibration permanently inert without it).

EXECUTION ORDER (parallel): TIER 1 (P1, parallel) -- US-320 pymysql. TIER 2 (P2, parallel) -- US-324 drive_statistics writer. TIER 3 (P3, parallel) -- US-321 sqlite fallback + US-322 orphan cleanup + US-323 backfill. Five independent stories; no inter-dependency.

THEME: 'Server analytics pipeline unblocked + data hygiene closed.' V0.27 chain has been writing data successfully (V0.27.4 US-315 sync UPDATE has FIRST IRL evidence via drain_event_id=16 closing on both Pi and server). V0.27.6 fixes the remaining gaps in the analytics pipeline so when Drive 11+ post-B-063 happens, the FULL chain produces: drive_summary INSERT/UPDATE -> sync to server -> drive_statistics writer fires -> calibration CLI runs (with pymysql + correct fallback) -> baselines populate.

CONSTRAINTS (anti-blocker discipline + Sprint 28-31 lessons): (a) US-274 file-existence check ran during grooming. (b) US-282 commit-vs-claim verifier active. (c) BL-010 + verify-table-names rule applied. (d) feedback_pm_verify_diagnostic_premises.md: PM reproduced each premise empirically -- pymysql missing confirmed via `python -c 'import pymysql'`; sqlite fallback verified via reading scripts/report.py:89; drive_statistics writer absence confirmed via `rg INSERT INTO drive_statistics` returning 1 test-file hit only. (e) feedback_pm_run_pre_flight_during_grooming.md: PM ran pre-flight for all 5 stories during grooming. (f) feedback_pm_main_merges_at_chain_end_only.md: V0.27.6 stays on sprint branch; chain doesn't merge until validated. (g) feedback_pm_validate_cli_in_cio_shell.md: US-320 + US-321 CLI fixes MUST be validated from CIO's local Windows shell context, not just server-side. (h) Per CIO 2026-05-11 Option A implicit on Story B (sqlite fallback removal; PM + Spool joint recommendation).

GROUNDING SOURCES: Spool 2026-05-11 calibration-CLI audit note (5 stories + drain_event_id=16 first US-315 IRL evidence) + PM 2026-05-11 verification (pymysql missing + scripts/report.py:92-95 + drive_statistics writer absence via rg). Plus V0.27.5 chain-tip state (V0.27 chain on stacked sprint branches awaiting Drive 11+ + B-063).

POST-SPRINT-32 ACTION ITEMS (NOT in sprint.json per CIO no-human-tasks rule): (1) CIO completes B-063 fuse-box wiring (still gates Drive 11+ for V0.27 chain IRL validation). (2) CIO validates US-320 + US-321 locally from Windows shell (per feedback_pm_validate_cli_in_cio_shell.md). (3) Drive 11+ post-fuse-box validates the full pipeline: drive_summary -> sync UPDATE -> drive_statistics writer (US-324) -> calibration CLI. (4) B-066 B-047 self-update IRL drill gates chain merge. (5) Once V0.27 chain fully validated, CIO invokes /chain-validated (V0.27.5 US-318) to merge V0.27 chain to main.

stories.title: Add pymysql to requirements-server.txt (I-022, Spool Story A)
stories.intent: I-022: scripts/report.py rewrites async DATABASE_URL to sync via _toSyncDriverUrl() (line 95) using mysql+pymysql://. SQLAlchemy then needs pymysql Python package to drive MariaDB synchronously. pymysql is NOT declared in requirements-server.txt -- only aiomysql>=0.2.0 is. EVERY CLI report path (--drive / --trends / --calibrate) crashes with ModuleNotFoundError on a clean venv. Mike has been doing all MariaDB ad-hoc work via the mysql binary CLI (separate from pymysql Python library), so the gap stayed silent. Fix: add pymysql>=1.1.0 to requirements-server.txt. Same dependency-declaration anti-pattern class as historical npm/pip 'works on my machine' gaps.

filesToTouch: requirements-server.txt (UPDATE -- add `pymysql>=1.1.0` line alongside existing aiomysql; ordering before aiomysql for alphabetical consistency; mod-history comment cites US-320 + I-022)
tests/server/test_requirements_pymysql_declared.py (NEW -- regression test asserts pymysql is in requirements-server.txt; would FAIL pre-fix because the line doesn't exist)
filesToRead: offices/pm/issues/I-022-pymysql-missing-from-requirements.md (full bug spec + architecture distinction: MariaDB server vs mysql CLI binary vs pymysql Python library)
requirements-server.txt existing entries (aiomysql>=0.2.0 is the async sibling)
doNotTouch: scripts/report.py business logic (only the dependency declaration is missing; the script itself is correct)
aiomysql declaration (async path; still required for fastapi server)
deploy-server.sh (pip install -r requirements-server.txt will pick up new line automatically)

groundingRefs.value: Spool 2026-05-11 audit: python scripts/report.py --calibrate --device chi-eclipse-01 crashes with ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'pymysql' at SQLAlchemy engine creation
PM 2026-05-11 verified: `grep ^pymysql requirements-server.txt` returns empty; `python -c 'import pymysql'` ModuleNotFoundError on local Windows env

Real-world validation gate: post-V0.27.6 deploy + clean venv pip install -r requirements-server.txt + python scripts/report.py --calibrate runs past import (downstream 'need 5 real drives' is OK -- different gate); plus CIO local Windows validation (feedback_pm_validate_cli_in_cio_shell.md)

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: grep `^pymysql|^aiomysql` requirements-server.txt -- confirm only aiomysql present; rg `+pymysql://|import pymysql` scripts/ src/ -- confirm pymysql is needed at scripts/report.py:95 only
requirements-server.txt has `pymysql>=1.1.0` line; mod-history entry added
tests/server/test_requirements_pymysql_declared.py: asserts the line exists; would FAIL pre-fix
verification: pytest tests/ -m 'not slow' -q (full fast suite GREEN; no broken imports)

Clean-venv smoke: `python -m venv /tmp/test-venv && /tmp/test-venv/bin/pip install -r requirements-server.txt && /tmp/test-venv/bin/python -c 'import pymysql; print(pymysql.__version__)'` -- prints 1.1.x or later
invariants: Synthetic test FAILS pre-fix (line absent)
Real-world validation gate: clean-venv install includes pymysql; scripts/report.py invocation no longer fails on import
aiomysql declaration unchanged (async server path unaffected)
Version pin >= 1.1.0 (SQLAlchemy 2.x compatible per Spool research)
stopConditions: If pre-flight reveals pymysql is referenced elsewhere with a DIFFERENT version pin requirement (e.g., a transitive dep that needs 1.0.x) -- STOP, document; PM resolves version conflict before merging the add

stories.title: Remove phantom sqlite fallback in report.py (I-023)
stories.intent: I-023: scripts/report.py:89 defines _DEFAULT_DB_URL_FALLBACK pointing at data/server_crawl.db which exists but has empty schema. Any CLI command using the fallback (when DATABASE_URL unset + --db-url omitted) crashes with sqlite3.OperationalError: no such table: drive_summary. Production never hits this (DATABASE_URL is set) but it's a confusing footgun for ad-hoc testing. Per CIO 2026-05-11 implicit Option A (PM + Spool joint recommendation): remove the fallback entirely; make --db-url or DATABASE_URL required. Cleaner failure mode; no phantom.

filesToTouch: scripts/report.py (UPDATE -- remove _DEFAULT_DB_URL_FALLBACK constant at line 89; update _resolveDbUrl to raise SystemExit(2) with clear error message when neither --db-url nor DATABASE_URL env var present; update CLI help text)
tests/scripts/test_report_db_url_resolution.py (NEW OR UPDATE -- assert CLI exits non-zero with clear error when no DB URL provided; would FAIL pre-fix because fallback silently returns sqlite path)
filesToRead: data/server_crawl.db (existing empty-skeleton file; not removed by this story -- leave on disk to avoid confusing future ad-hoc work)
doNotTouch: data/server_crawl.db file itself (leave on disk; just remove the silent fallback that points at it)
scripts/load_data.py (if someone wants local-sqlite path back, this is the seeder path)
DATABASE_URL env var handling (semantics unchanged; only fallback removed)

groundingRefs.value: Spool 2026-05-11 audit: unset DATABASE_URL + python scripts/report.py --drive 8 crashes with sqlite3.OperationalError: no such table: drive_summary -- fallback fires + sqlite has empty schema
PM + Spool joint recommendation: Option A (remove fallback) over Option B (probe + clean error) or Option C (defer as TD). Cleaner failure mode; production never depends on fallback
Real-world validation gate: CIO runs `python scripts/report.py --drive N` from local Windows shell with no env vars set; clear error printed; non-zero exit (feedback_pm_validate_cli_in_cio_shell.md)

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: rg `_DEFAULT_DB_URL_FALLBACK|server_crawl.db` scripts/ -- map all references; confirm only scripts/report.py uses the constant
_DEFAULT_DB_URL_FALLBACK constant removed from scripts/report.py
_resolveDbUrl raises SystemExit(2) with message like 'DATABASE_URL not set; pass --db-url or export DATABASE_URL' when neither provided
tests/scripts/test_report_db_url_resolution.py: asserts SystemExit(2) + error message when no DB URL provided; would FAIL pre-fix
invariants: Synthetic test FAILS pre-fix (fallback silently returns sqlite path; no SystemExit raised)
Real-world validation gate: CIO local Windows invocation with no DATABASE_URL prints clear error + exits non-zero
With DATABASE_URL set OR --db-url passed, CLI behavior unchanged
stopConditions: If pre-flight reveals fallback IS used by an existing test fixture or workflow -- STOP, document; PM evaluates whether to keep + improve (Option B) or migrate the consumer to explicit --db-url

stories.title: Pi realtime_data NULL-drive_id orphan cleanup (B-072)
stories.intent: B-072: Spool 2026-05-11 audit found 61,293 NULL-drive_id rows in Pi-side realtime_data accumulated since Session 10's cleanup. Sources: reconnect-loop polling (engine-off), I-019 DriveDetector warm-restart gap, pre-DriveDetector grace period. Not poison (Spool's tuning filters on drive_id IS NOT NULL) but wasteful + grows unbounded. Approach 1 (recommended): periodic cleanup task via systemd timer; deletes NULL-drive_id rows older than 24h. Idempotent + logged.

filesToTouch: scripts/cleanup_orphan_realtime_data.py (NEW -- stdlib script; deletes realtime_data rows where drive_id IS NULL AND timestamp_ms < NOW - 24h; --dry-run + --execute flags; logs row count before/after; idempotent)
deploy/orphan-cleanup.service + deploy/orphan-cleanup.timer (NEW -- systemd unit + timer; runs nightly at 03:00 local; sync-if-changed install pattern mirrors drain-forensics units)

tests/scripts/test_cleanup_orphan_realtime_data.py (NEW -- seeds synthetic DB with mix of NULL-old + NULL-recent + valid drive-tagged rows; asserts cleanup removes only NULL-old; would FAIL pre-fix because the script doesn't exist)
filesToRead: offices/pm/backlog/B-072-pi-realtime-data-orphan-cleanup.md (full bug spec + Approach 1 vs Approach 2 trade-off)
src/pi/obdii/database_schema.py existing realtime_data table definition (column types, indexes; need to ensure cleanup query is efficient)
deploy/drain-forensics.service + drain-forensics.timer (pattern reference for systemd unit + timer install)
doNotTouch: Writer-side path (Approach 2 is deferred; this story is Approach 1 cleanup-only)
Pi-side schema (cleanup is DELETE-only; no schema change)

groundingRefs.value: Spool 2026-05-11 audit: Pi-side realtime_data has 61,293 NULL-drive_id rows (counted via sqlite3 SELECT COUNT)

Approach 1 recommended (script + nightly timer) over Approach 2 (writer guard) per Spool: simpler, reversible, addresses symptom without risking legitimate pre-drive-start rows being dropped at write time

Real-world validation gate: post-V0.27.6 deploy + 48h running -> Pi-side `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM realtime_data WHERE drive_id IS NULL` returns substantially lower number (close to 0 modulo recent <24h rows)

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: sqlite3 data/obd.db `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM realtime_data WHERE drive_id IS NULL` -- record baseline count for post-deploy comparison
scripts/cleanup_orphan_realtime_data.py executes cleanly with --dry-run + --execute; deletes only NULL-drive_id rows older than 24h
Systemd unit + timer installed via deploy-pi.sh; nightly run scheduled at 03:00 local
tests/scripts/test_cleanup_orphan_realtime_data.py: seeded synthetic DB + cleanup run + assertion; would FAIL pre-fix
invariants: Synthetic test FAILS pre-fix (script absent)
Real-world validation gate: post-V0.27.6 deploy + 48h, Pi-side NULL-drive_id row count drops substantially (close to 0 modulo recent <24h)
Cleanup is idempotent: re-running on already-clean DB is a no-op
Cleanup logs row count before/after (observable in journalctl)
stopConditions: If pre-flight reveals NULL-drive_id rows being USED by some downstream consumer (e.g., a debug query) -- STOP, document; PM decides whether to migrate consumer or extend retention
If systemd timer interval conflicts with existing scheduled jobs -- STOP, propose alternative window

stories.title: Server battery_health_log backfill -- stranded rows 11-15 (B-073)
stories.intent: B-073: V0.27.4 US-315 added sync UPDATE propagation (modified_at cursor) and works forward (drain_event_id=16 closed cleanly on both Pi and server post-V0.27.4 deploy -- first IRL evidence). But US-315 does NOT auto-replay missed historical UPDATEs. Server-side battery_health_log rows 11-15 are stranded with end_timestamp=NULL despite Pi-side having full close-event data. One-off SQL UPDATE backfills them. Recommended over generic reconciliation script -- simpler + faster + addresses concrete known gap.

filesToTouch: scripts/backfill_server_battery_health_log_stranded.py (NEW -- stdlib script; reads Pi-side authoritative data via SSH for drain_event_ids 11-15; emits MariaDB UPDATE statements server-side; --dry-run + --execute; idempotent; logs row-by-row diff)

tests/scripts/test_backfill_server_battery_health_log_stranded.py (NEW -- mocks Pi-side data + server-side state; asserts script generates correct UPDATE SQL + skips already-populated rows; would FAIL pre-fix because the script doesn't exist)
filesToRead: offices/pm/backlog/B-073-server-battery-health-log-backfill-stranded.md (full bug spec + Option 1 vs Option 2 analysis)
doNotTouch: US-315 modified_at cursor logic (forward path; works correctly)
Pi-side authoritative data (script READS but does NOT modify)
Server-side row PKs / source_id (only end_timestamp + end_soc + end_vcell_v + runtime_seconds fields updated)

groundingRefs.value: Spool 2026-05-11 audit: drain_event_ids 11-15 server-side end_timestamp=NULL; Pi-side populated; US-315 forward-only (no replay)
Option 1 (one-off SQL) chosen over Option 2 (generic reconciliation) per Spool: trivial mechanical fix for 5 known rows; generic script deferred until Drive 11+ validates drive_summary side too
Real-world validation gate: post-V0.27.6 + script run -> server-side battery_health_log rows 11-15 show end_timestamp + end_soc + end_vcell_v + runtime_seconds matching Pi-side authoritative state

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: SSH chi-srv-01 + `mysql obd2db -e 'SELECT id, end_timestamp FROM battery_health_log WHERE id IN (11,12,13,14,15)'` -- confirm current NULL state baseline
scripts/backfill_server_battery_health_log_stranded.py executes via --dry-run + --execute; populates rows 11-15 server-side with values matching Pi-side
Script idempotent: re-running on already-populated rows is a no-op (UPDATE only fires when end_timestamp IS NULL)
tests/scripts/test_backfill_server_battery_health_log_stranded.py: mocks + asserts script generates correct SQL + skips populated rows; would FAIL pre-fix
invariants: Synthetic test FAILS pre-fix (script absent)
Real-world validation gate: post-script-run, server rows 11-15 match Pi-side state
Script idempotent (UPDATEs only NULL fields; no overwrites)
Script READ-ONLY against Pi-side (no Pi modifications)
stopConditions: If Pi-side authoritative data for rows 11-15 is somehow unavailable (e.g., Pi DB reset) -- STOP, document; mark these 5 rows permanently stranded + close story
If server-side schema has changed since the stranded rows were INSERTed (column rename, type change) -- STOP, audit + propose schema-aware backfill

stories.title: drive_statistics writer -- build production writer (I-024)
stories.intent: I-024: Spool 2026-05-11 audit + PM verification confirmed NO production code writes to drive_statistics table. Schema exists; proposeCalibration() joins through it for baselines; but writer is missing. Without this, calibration permanently returns 'Need 5 more real drives' regardless of how many drives complete. This is the missing link between drive_summary (V0.27.3 US-310 + V0.27.4 US-317) and calibration. Build the writer: server-side computed at sync-time (post-drive_summary INSERT) emits per-parameter aggregates (mean/min/max/stddev) for canonical PIDs (RPM, COOLANT_TEMP, SPEED, IAT, MAF, TPS, LTFT_1, STFT_1, TIMING_ADVANCE, BAT_V). One drive_statistics row per (drive_id, parameter_name).

filesToTouch: src/server/services/analysis.py (UPDATE -- add _ensureDriveStatistics() function called after _ensureDriveSummary; computes per-parameter aggregates from realtime_data for the drive_id; inserts drive_statistics rows; honors Spec 3 _PRESERVE_ON_UPDATE for idempotency)
src/server/db/models.py (UPDATE if needed -- verify DriveStatistic ORM model exists + matches schema; if missing, add)

tests/server/test_drive_statistics_writer.py (NEW -- mocks drive_summary INSERT + realtime_data rows; asserts drive_statistics rows written with correct aggregates; covers canonical PIDs + edge cases (drive too short / single-row drive / all-NULL parameter); would FAIL pre-fix because writer doesn't exist)

scripts/backfill_drive_statistics.py (NEW -- one-shot backfill for drives 3-10 if data quality permits; reads realtime_data + computes aggregates + inserts drive_statistics rows; --dry-run + --execute; idempotent)

Real-world validation gate: post-V0.27.6 deploy + Drive 11+ produces drive_statistics rows on server (one per (drive_id, parameter_name) combo for canonical PIDs); backfill for drives 3-10 fills historical state where data quality permits
